featuresMetaData.featuresDescription: Discover the powerful features that make up the Riavzon security and authentication infrastructure.

features.title: Centralized Identity Management
features.description: Complete signup, login, and comprehensive user session management providing a robust single source of truth for your applications.

features.title: Advanced Bot Defense
features.description: Multi-layered traffic analysis that intelligently identifies and blocks sophisticated scrapers while permitting known search engines.

features.title: Robust Token Architecture
features.description: Enterprise-grade JWT security utilizing long-lived hashed refresh tokens alongside fast, stateless, short-lived access tokens.

features.title: Passwordless Auth & MFA
features.description: Enhance user security and experience with built-in support for secure magic links and email-based Multi-Factor Authentication.

features.title: Dynamic Traffic Filtering
features.description: Protect downstream endpoints with granular IP validation, deep GeoIP tracking, proxy detection, and customizable penalty scoring.

features.title: Seamless OAuth
features.description: Pre-configured third-party authentication flows that synchronize natively and effortlessly with your core user database.

features.title: Persistent Rate Limiting
features.description: Mitigate brute-force attacks via comprehensive multi-tiered burst and sustained rate limiting, backed securely by MySQL.

features.title: Native Framework Support
features.description: Drop-in caching, routing, middlewares, and strict utilities designed specifically to integrate with Nuxt, Nitro, and Express.

features.title: HMAC Request Sealing
features.description: Ensure total integrity and authenticity of server-to-server requests originating from the edge gateway using HMAC-SHA256 signatures.

features.title: Mutual TLS (mTLS)
features.description: Enforce rigorous enterprise security standards by requiring bi-directional cryptographic identity validation for internal network traffic.
